headline: Solstice USX: Solana yield-bearing stablecoin with Halborn audit; specific program addresses not publicly discoverable
short_headline: USX protocol identified; program addresses undiscovered

findings.text: No specific Solana program addresses surfaced from public block explorers or name-tag searches; protocol confirms contracts exist but addresses not publicly indexed

Official docs site (docs.solstice.finance) inaccessible; main website (solstice.finance) confirms USX Program and YieldVault exist but does not publish deployment addresses; no dedicated 'deployments' page located

Halborn audit report URL in pinned context (storage.googleapis.com/dapp_prod/audit_reports/halborn_program_audit_250605.pdf) inaccessible; no alternative audit PDF URLs surfaced; Halborn case study confirms engagement but does not disclose in-scope addresses
GitHub repository not recorded in protocol metadata; GitHub topic search returned unrelated projects; no Solstice Finance source repository discovered
Protocol on single chain (Solana); address discovery blocked by lack of public deployment documentation
Not applicable; no factory addresses discovered to enumerate children
Administrative structure identified: Squads multisig controls all programs with upgrade authority; specific multisig address not disclosed
No addresses currently eligible for admin_addresses[] without on-chain discovery; Squads multisig governance mentioned but address not publicly available in fetched sources

rationale.verdict: Solstice USX confirmed as operational Solana protocol with institutional backing (Deus X Capital), Halborn security audit, and Squads multisig governance. However, the specific Solana program addresses for USX, YieldVault, and governance contracts remain undiscovered despite searches of official website, documentation portals, GitHub, and block-explorer name tags. The audit PDF and deployed contracts documentation are inaccessible. Grade is unknown because canonical on-chain addresses could not be recovered from fetched sources; next runs inherit the current address_book (null) and should attempt Solana block-explorer direct searches, contact the team for addresses, or retrieve addresses from subsequent audit/docs accessibility.

evidence.shows: Confirms USX Program handles minting/redemption with 1:1 collateralization, YieldVault allows staking USX for eUSX, all programs governed by Squads multisig
Halborn conducted full smart contract audit of Solstice including USX Program and YieldVault; confirms mainnet launch preparation
Confirms Halborn audits employed, Squads multisig controls, multi-signature controls and real-time proof-of-reserves implemented

Confirms Halborn audit of smart contracts including USX program; Ceffu and Copper custody partners; Chainlink Proof of Reserve; Squads multisig governance; CEO Ben Nadareski; 30+ team members from crypto/TradFi
Confirms official launch September 30, 2025; Deus X Capital backing; $160M+ TVL; institutional investor backing (Galaxy Digital, MEV Capital, Bitcoin Suisse, Auros); SLX native utility token planned

unknowns: D1: Solana block-explorer name-tag search and address discovery not completed; specific USX Program, YieldVault, and governance contract addresses not surfaced
D2: Canonical deployed_contracts_doc URL not accessible; docs.solstice.finance offline; no alternative deployments listing page located
D3: Audit PDF at pinned URL (storage.googleapis.com/dapp_prod/audit_reports/halborn_program_audit_250605.pdf) inaccessible; no in-scope contract addresses extracted
D4: GitHub repository for Solstice Finance not found; no source-code deployment artifacts (broadcast/, deployments/, addresses.json) discoverable
D8: Squads multisig governance address not publicly available in fetched sources; unable to populate admin_addresses[] without on-chain program address

protocol_metadata.about: <cite index="1-7,1-12">Solstice is a public, permissionless protocol unlocking secure, institutional-grade yield on Solana, introducing USX, a fully collateralized settlement layer backed by USDC and USDT</cite>. <cite index="2-30,2-31">The YieldVault lets users stake USX and receive eUSX, a yield-bearing token, with rewards distributed via epoch-based logic with atomic mint/burn enforcement</cite>. <cite index="2-32">All programs are governed by Squads multisig with upgrade support</cite>. <cite index="15-14">The protocol is backed by Deus X Capital, a $1 billion digital asset investment firm</cite>.
